articles.title: John Ternus is taking over from Tim Cook as Apple’s CEO
articles.description:
John Ternus | Image: Apple	

The multitrillion-dollar home of iPhone, Mac, and so many other tech gadgets is getting a new leader this fall, as Apple has announced that Tim Cook will be replaced as CEO on September 1st by current hardware boss John Ternus. How will we look back on Tim Cook’s legacy, and what will Apple look like under the leadership of John Ternus and new chief hardware officer Johny Srouji?
We’ll keep track of all of the latest updates right here, starting with a live recording of the Vergecast.


					
Read Tim Cook’s letter to the Apple world as he departs as CEO
			
New Apple CEO calls for an emergency Vergecast.
			
Apple names Johny Srouji as chief hardware officer
			
Apple CEO Tim Cook is stepping down

articles.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook is stepping down, John Ternus set to replace him
articles.description: Apple CEO Tim Cook is stepping down, with the company announcing on Monday that John Ternus will take his place as the technology giant's leader.

articles.title: John Ternus will replace Tim Cook as Apple CEO
articles.description: Cook will be executive chairman, but will no longer run the company day to day.

articles.title: Trump’s Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer latest to leave administration
articles.description: Chavez-DeRemer leaves the Trump administration after the recent departures of Kristi Noem and Pam Bondi.

articles.title: Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer leaves Trump cabinet, Keith Sonderling takes over
articles.description: Department of Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer is resigning from the Trump administration, the White House said.

articles.title: Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer resigns
articles.description: Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer resigned, according to two sources with knowledge of the decision. Chavez-DeRemer is the third Cabinet member to leave their post during President Donald Trump's second term.

articles.title: Nvidia supplier Victory Giant sees shares soar 60% in Hong Kong debut
articles.description: Shares of Victory Giant Technology surged in what is Hong Kong's largest initial public offering debut so far this year.

articles.title: Victory Giant Surges in Debut After Biggest Hong Kong Listing This Year
articles.description: Victory Giant Technology Huizhou Co., a Chinese supplier of Nvidia Corp., surged 57% in its Hong Kong trading debut after raising $2.6 billion in the city’s largest listing in seven months.

articles.title: A Humanoid Robot Set a Half-Marathon Record in China
articles.description: An autonomous robot from the company Honor ran a half marathon in 50:26, beating the human record by 7 minutes.

articles.title: Robot named Lightning blows past human runners to set half-marathon record in Beijing
articles.description: Lightning, one of more than 100 robots running the Beijing E-Town, avoided a serious mishap and crossed the finish line in a record 50 minutes, 26 seconds.

articles.title: GT's middle order 'exposed', but the problem might be higher up
articles.description: "We shouldn't be allowing Rahul Tewatia or Shahrukh Khan [to face] lots of balls," Matthew Hayden, Gujarat Titans' batting coach, says after the collapse against Mumbai Indians

articles.title: Moni, Sharmin help Bangladesh register maiden ODI win against Sri Lanka
articles.description: Bangladesh lost four wickets for one run when they were close to the finish line, but averted disaster in the end

articles.title: Gill misses chance to top Orange Cap table, Rabada on the rise on Purple Cap list
articles.description: Here's what the Orange and Purple Cap tables at IPL 2026 look like after Mumbai Indians' dominant win over Gujarat Titans on Monday

articles.description: West Texas Intermediate and Brent had settled 7% and 5% higher on Monday.

dailyNews.title: Trump and Iran Face Off in Iran War Negotiations
dailyNews.description: As the United States and Iran make a second attempt at a deal, their negotiating styles are on a collision course.

dailyNews.title: Trump and Iran trade conflicting messages over prospect of talks
dailyNews.description: Iran balked at President Trump's demands for a peace deal amid a standoff over the Strait of Hormuz, as fear the war could reignite hits oil and energy markets.

dailyNews.title: Businesses start requests for Trump tariff refunds, but glitches reported in new portal
dailyNews.description: Beginning on Monday, businesses were able to apply for refunds for President Trump's tariffs that were later declared unconstitutional by the Supreme Court. Jo Ling Kent reports on some of the issues they're running into.

dailyNews.title: How Trump's messaging on Iran has shifted since saying they "agreed to everything"
dailyNews.description: Less than 48 hours after President Trump said Iran has "agreed to everything," he threatened the whole country would get "blown up" without a deal soon.

dailyNews.title: Mixed signals from Trump on state of talks with Iran
dailyNews.description: On Friday, President Trump was saying Iran had "agreed to everything," including giving up its nuclear program "forever." But by Monday, he had accused Iran of violating the ceasefire and warned that if a deal isn't reached, "then lots of bombs start going off." Ed O'Keefe reports.

dailyNews.title: Former Kennedy Center curator says he believes "real reason" behind closure is "financial decline"
dailyNews.description: In a recent article in The Atlantic, former Kennedy Center curator Josef Palermo revealed what he observed during his 10 months there as President Trump tightened his grip on the cultural institution. Palermo joins CBS News to discuss.

dailyNews.title: Some businesses report issues with Trump administration's new tariff refund portal
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration launched an online portal on Monday to begin refunding businesses impacted by tariffs the Supreme Court deemed illegal, but some have already reported issues with the system. CBS News MoneyWatch reporter Megan Cerullo has the details.

dailyNews.title: State of play in Strait of Hormuz as ceasefire deadline nears
dailyNews.description: President Trump said Monday an agreement with Iran could come quickly, but at the same time, he feels "no pressure" to strike a deal. CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean joins to discuss the state of play in the Strait of Hormuz.

dailyNews.title: Trump ‘unlikely’ to extend ceasefire as deadline for deal approaches
dailyNews.description: President Trump said he was “unlikely” to extend the ceasefire as he pushed the Iranian regime to agree to a nuclear deal. NBC News’ Garret Haake reports.

dailyNews.title: Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer resigns
dailyNews.description: Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer resigned, according to two sources with knowledge of the decision. Chavez-DeRemer is the third Cabinet member to leave their post during President Donald Trump's second term.

dailyNews.title: Bill Nye blasts proposed cuts to NASA's budget
dailyNews.description: NBC News' Sahil Kapur speaks to Bill Nye about the White House's 2027 budget proposal, which threatens to cut 23% of NASA's funding.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says Colombia Will Accept Deportees, Ending Tariff Standoff
dailyNews.description: The president has made immigration an early priority to start second term, with ICE making more than 900 arrests Sunday.
